methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The virtual price of an LP share must be monotonically non-decreasing to prevent yield theft.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the virtual price decreases, LPs lose value. An attacker could manipulate pool balances via swaps or rounding errors to extract value at the expense of existing LPs.
invariants.invariant_logic: post.ValuePerShare >= pre.ValuePerShare, where ValuePerShare = (reserveValidatorToken + (reserveUserToken * N) / SCALE) / totalSupply
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_virtual_price_monotonicity(bytes32 poolId) public view { assert(currentValuePerShare(poolId) >= previousValuePerShare[poolId]); }

invariants.description: The contract's actual ERC20 balance for any token must be greater than or equal to the sum of all pool reserves for that token.
invariants.attack_scenario: If reserves exceed actual balances (e.g., due to fee-on-transfer tokens or direct transfers out), the contract becomes insolvent, leading to a bank run where the last LPs cannot withdraw their funds.
invariants.invariant_logic: IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(this)) >= sum(pool.reserveUserToken) + sum(pool.reserveValidatorToken) for all pools where token is used
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_reserve_solvency(address token) public view { assert(IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(this)) >= getTotalReservesForToken(token)); }

invariants.description: Total supply of LP tokens must equal the sum of all user liquidity balances plus the locked MIN_LIQUIDITY.
invariants.attack_scenario: If total supply desyncs from user balances, the proportional withdrawal math in `burn` will calculate incorrect amounts, either trapping funds in the contract or allowing users to withdraw more than their fair share.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_total_supply_conservation(bytes32 poolId) public view { assert(totalSupply[poolId] == getTotalLiquidityBalances(poolId) + (totalSupply[poolId] > 0 ? MIN_LIQUIDITY : 0)); }

invariants.description: A round-trip swap must result in a net loss for the user, ensuring no risk-free arbitrage can drain the pool.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a round-trip swap is profitable (due to fee multiplier misconfiguration or rounding errors), an attacker can use a flash loan to repeatedly cycle funds, draining the pool's reserves entirely.
invariants.invariant_logic: rebalanceSwap_amountIn(X) > executeFeeSwap_amountOut(X) for any amount X
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_no_free_arbitrage(uint256 amount) public pure { assert(((amount * N) / SCALE + 1) > ((amount * M) / SCALE)); }

invariants.description: Once initialized, a pool's total supply must never fall below MIN_LIQUIDITY.
invariants.attack_scenario: If total supply can reach zero after initialization, an attacker can reset the pool and perform an inflation attack (ERC4626-style) on the next depositor, stealing their deposited liquidity.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_min_liquidity_lock(bytes32 poolId) public view { assert(totalSupply[poolId] == 0 || totalSupply[poolId] >= MIN_LIQUIDITY); }
